description_long: This crustacean features a segmented, elongated body with a pronounced cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen. Its exoskeleton appears semi-translucent and textured, with a muted, grayscale coloration. The creature is supported by six articulated legs, each jointed and angled for mobility, and it possesses two prominent claws at the front. Two long, thin antennae extend from the head, adding to its sensory capabilities. The eyes are large and positioned at the front of the head, giving it a forward-facing gaze. The overall form is insect-like, with a slightly angular, polygonal surface finish.
description: A segmented, semi-translucent crustacean with six legs, two claws, long antennae, and a tapered body. Its surface is textured and grayscale, with prominent eyes and articulated limbs.
description_view_0: Front view shows the face, large eyes, two claws, and both antennae clearly extending forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length of the abdomen, the angular segmentation, and the articulation of the legs and claws.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full taper of the abdomen, the symmetry of the legs, and the absence of facial features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the other profile, showing the claws, legs, and the layered body segments.
